title: Buttery Tender Vietnamese Chicken Bun Bo Hue
description:
Crispy buttery chicken served in a flavorful spicy broth, served with soft bun, fresh herbs, and cucumber slices.

This is a popular Vietnamese dish from Hue, often served as a family-style meal.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the ginger and garlic and saut0e until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chicken drumsticks and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the yakitori butter and stir until melted and fragrant.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and chili flakes, and stir to combine.
Pour in 4 cups of chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Meanwhile, grill the cucumber slices on the yakimono-grill until charred and slightly softened.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the chicken and broth into a bowl, then top with grilled cucumber slices, chopped cilantro, and serve with soft bun on the side.
